summary: A collection of X (Twitter) posts by Christopher Manning where he expresses skepticism about near-term AGI timelines, criticizes Effective Altruism-driven AI doomerism, and comments on the intersection of machine learning and traditional algorithms.

principles.statement: Human-level AI is not close at hand.
principles.rationale: Historical predictions of imminent AGI have been consistently wrong, and current rhetoric mirrors the overly optimistic claims of the 1970s.
principles.source_quote: I do not believe human-level AI (artificial superintelligence, or the commonest sense of \ ) is close at hand. AI has made breakthroughs, but the claim of AGI by 2030 is as laughable as claims of AGI by 1980 are in retrospect.

principles.statement: America should not overestimate its exclusivity in AI breakthroughs.
principles.rationale: While the US is the center of the AI industry, claiming every major breakthrough is American is an overstatement.
principles.source_quote: America does itself no favors when it overestimates its specialness.

signature_quotes.text: I do not believe human-level AI (artificial superintelligence, or the commonest sense of \ ) is close at hand.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing AGI timelines and pushing back against claims of AGI by 2030.

signature_quotes.text: Machine Learning just ate Algorithms in one large bite...
signature_quotes.context: Commenting on a 2017 Google paper about learned index structures replacing traditional algorithms.

signature_quotes.text: America does itself no favors when it overestimates its specialness.
signature_quotes.context: Responding to claims that every major AI breakthrough is American.

anti_patterns.description: Believing in near-term AGI timelines (e.g., AGI by 2030).
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It ignores the historical reality that similar rhetoric in the past (like in the 1970s) proved completely wrong.

anti_patterns.description: Making governance or leadership decisions based on 'EA-infused fantasy lands' or AI doomerism.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It leads to irrational fears, such as believing there is a 50/50 chance everyone gets 'paperclipped & dies', which undermines sound leadership.
